person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
"Philip the Bold was Duke of Burgundy and jure uxoris Count of Flanders, Artois and Burgundy. The fourth and youngest son of King John II of France and his wife, Bonne of Luxembourg, Philip was the founder of the Burgundian branch of the House of Valois. His vast collection of territories made him the undisputed premier peer of the kingdom of France and made his successors formidable subjects, and sometimes rivals, of the kings of France."

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philip_the_Bold

French Royalty. Duke of Burgundy, also known as Philip the Bold and as Philip II of Bourgogne. He was the younger son of King of France John II "the Good" and Bonne of Luxembourg. In 1369 he married Margaret of Dampierre, widow of Philip de Rouvre and heiress of Flanders. After his death, the corpse was embalmed and the heart was sent to Paris to be buried with his ancestors at St. Denis. On May 1, 1404 the body was transferred to Dijon accompanied by sixty official mourners, Philip’s sons, John the Fearless and Antoine De Valois, Duke of Brabant, with two counts and six leading Flemish nobles. Leading members of Philips household and administration as well as sixteen chaplains also escorted the company. On June 1 the cortege reached Dijon, where it was greeted by 100 burgesses and 100 poor men all dressed in black, along with all the clergy of Dijon. On June 16 the Duke was finally laid to rest in the choir, at the Charterhouse.

Margaret and Philip had the following children:
1 John I of Burgundy, Duke of Burgundy (1371-1419, murdered at Montereau), her eldest son and successor in Flanders, Artois, and Burgundy.
2 Charles of Burgundy (1372\endash 1373)
3 Margaret of Burgundy (October 1374-March 8, 1441, Le Quesnoy), married William II, Duke of Bavaria.
4 Louis of Burgundy (1377-1378)
5 Catherine of Burgundy (April 1378, Montbard-January 24, 1425, Gray, Haute-Saône), married Leopold IV, Duke of Austria.
6 Bonne of Burgundy (1379-1399, Arras)
7 Mary of Burgundy (September 1380, Dijon-October 2, 1422, Thonon-les-Bains), married Amadeus VIII, Duke of Savoy.
8 Antoine of Burgundy, Duke of Brabant (August, 1384-October 25, 1415, at the Battle of Agincourt.)
9 Philip II, Count of Nevers and Rethel (1389-1415, at the Battle of Agincourt.)

zh.lifeSummary: 當Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne於1342年1月17日出生在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾阿爾培-德-豪-普洛福斯朋托時，他的父親Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France22歲，母親Bonne de Luxembourg26歲。他於1369年6月19日與Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre在法國瑟-吉瓦朋托結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1404年4月27日在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特哈莉享年62歲，埋葬在法國布格-福朗-可特艾o-達迪喬。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne于1342年1月17日出生在法国普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾阿爾培-德-豪-普洛福斯朋托时，他的父亲Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France22岁，母亲Bonne de Luxembourg26岁。他于1369年6月19日与Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre在法国瑟-吉瓦朋托结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1404年4月27日在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特哈莉享年62岁，埋葬在法国布格-福朗-可特艾o-達迪喬。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourgogne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Bourgoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphee, Alyre, Andre, Armand, Aurore, Celina, Emile, Gilberte, Jacques, Laurent, Lucienne, Olivier.

variant of Bourgouin (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a habitational name for someone from Burgundy (Old French Bourgogne), a region of eastern France having Dijon as its center. The area was invaded by the Burgundii, an ancient Germanic tribe from whom it takes its name, in about AD 480. The duchy of Burgundy, created in 877 by Charles II, King of the Western Franks, was extremely powerful in the later Middle Ages, especially under Philip the Bold (1342–1404; duke from 1363). Compare Bourgoyne and Bourguignon .

nameMeanings.name: Philippe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Philip
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne was born on 17 January 1342, in Pontoise, Alpes-de-Haute-Provence, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, his father, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, was 22 and his mother, Bonne de Luxembourg, was 26. He married Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre on 19 June 1369, in Pontoise, Seine-et-Oise, France. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 27 April 1404, in Halle, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, at the age of 62, and was buried in Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne est né le 17 janvier 1342, à Pontoise, Alpes-de-Haute-Provence, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, son père, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Bonne de Luxembourg, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre le 19 juin 1369, à Pontoise, Seine-et-Oise, France. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 27 avril 1404, à Halle, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne am 17. Januar 1342 in Pontoise, Alpes-de-Haute-Provence, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France 22 und seine Mutter Bonne de Luxembourg 26. Er hat am 19. Juni 1369 in Pontoise, Seine-et-Oise, Frankreich Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 27. April 1404 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Halle, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien und wurde in Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne è nato il il 17 gennaio 1342 nel Pontoise, Alpes-de-Haute-Provence, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia, il suo padre, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Bonne de Luxembourg, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre il 19 giugno 1369, nel Pontoise, Seine-et-Oise, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 27 aprile 1404, nel Halle, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogneが1342年1月17日にフランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルアルペス-デ-ハウテ-プローベンスポントイゼで生まれた時，父Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de Franceは22歳，母Bonne de Luxembourgは26歳であった。1369年6月19日にフランスセアイネ-エト-オイゼポントイゼでMarguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1404年4月27日にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントハルで62歳で死去し，フランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeクoテ-ド'オルディジョンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne(이)가 1342년 1월 17일 프랑스 플로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 알페스-데-하테-플로븐스 폰토이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Bonne de Luxembourg(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1369년 6월 19일 프랑스 세이인-트-이스 폰토이스에서 Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1404년 4월 27일 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 핼일에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e co테-드'어 디조에 묻혔다.

pt.Hal, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas: Hal, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne nasceu em 17 de janeiro de 1342, em Pontoise, Alpes-de-Haute-Provence, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França, seu pai, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Bonne de Luxembourg, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre em 19 de junho de 1369, em Pontoise, Seine-et-Oise, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de abril de 1404, em Halle, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne родился 17 января 1342 г. в местности понтоис, алпес-де-хауте-провенс, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Bonne de Luxembourg, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre 19 июня 1369 г. в местности понтоис, сине-ет-оис, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 27 апреля 1404 г. в местности холл, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности диджон, кoте-д'ор, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция.

es.Hal, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas: Hal, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philippe II de Bourgogne Duc de Bourgogne nació el 17 de enero de 1342, en Pontoise, Alpes-de-Haute-Provence, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia, su papá, Jean II "Le Bon" de France Roi de France, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Bonne de Luxembourg, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Marguerite III de Flandre Comtesse de Flandre el 19 de junio de 1369, en Pontoise, Seine-et-Oise, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 27 de abril de 1404, en Halle, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Dijon, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.
